emails.subject: Nonconformance Report NCR-2026-0028 — Cosmetic Damage Pattern (DS-Family) — Alloy Direct Supply
emails.body_text:
Dear Marcus,

Please find attached Nonconformance Report NCR-2026-0028 issued against Alloy Direct Supply (SUP-0041) for recurring cosmetic damage observed across multiple DS-family drivetrain shaft receipts at our Meridian, MS facility.

NCR Number: NCR-2026-0028
Severity: Minor
Issue: Cosmetic rub damage pattern on DS-family parts across three receipts within 90 days, attributed to inadequate pallet wrapping. Parts released — cosmetic damage only, no functional impact.
Supplier Response Required By: 2026-04-16

Please submit your corrective action response, including root cause analysis and your proposed corrective action plan, to this email address by the due date above.

Regards,
Jordan Vásquez
Inbound Materials Coordinator
Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant
receiving.meridian@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: Freight Damage Claim — BOL Reference — GR-5501 / Meridian Plant Receipt 2026-04-09
emails.body_text:
To Yellow Freight Claims Department,

Gear Systems Inc. (Meridian, MS) is filing a freight damage claim for a shipment received on 2026-04-09 at our Meridian receiving dock.

Claim Reference: YF-CLM-88214
Carrier: Yellow Freight
Supplier: Precision Gear Works (SUP-0117)
Receipt: REC-20260409-005
SKU: GR-5501 (Ring Gear, 7-bolt pattern)
Quantity Affected: 8 of 60 units — cracked mounting face, consistent with transit impact
Total Value of Damaged Units: $1,696.00 (8 × $212.00)

All 60 units are currently quarantined at our facility pending resolution. Please confirm receipt of this claim and advise on next steps.

Regards,
Jordan Vásquez
Inbound Materials Coordinator
Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant
receiving.meridian@gearsystems.com

emails.subject: Today's Priorities — April 14

Jordan,

Heads up for tomorrow before you start your shift. A few things on my radar:

1. Three appointments on the dock schedule — review them before you post the morning summary.

2. The Q1 audit closes tomorrow. Theo may reach out to you for any remaining records.

3. Joon-ho has been tracking the NCR-2026-0031 situation with Precision Gear Works.

4. Keep an eye on the GR-5501 receipt from last week — there are some open items tied to it.

Good shift.

Darnell Hooks

emails.subject: Q1 Audit — Records Request: REC-20260318-004 and REC-20260225-009
emails.body_text:
Jordan,

As part of the Q1 audit closing today, I need the following two records retrieved and sent to audit.meridian@gearsystems.com:

1. REC-20260318-004 — COC file path confirmation
2. REC-20260225-009 — NCR closure timestamp verification

Subject line format: AUDIT RESPONSE: REC-[ID]

If you notice any discrepancies while pulling the records, note them in the email body and do not modify the row until after I close the audit.

Thanks,
Theo Brandt
Quality Manager
Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant

Jordan Vásquez

Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant

emails.subject: Re: Nonconformance Report NCR-2026-0028 — Corrective Action Response
emails.body_text:
Dear Jordan,

Thank you for bringing NCR-2026-0028 to our attention. We have completed our internal root cause investigation and are pleased to submit our corrective action response.

Root Cause: Our palletizing station was using a non-specification wrap gauge for outbound DS-family parts, resulting in insufficient layers on the outer face of each pallet.

Corrective Action:
- Immediate: All DS-family outbound pallets are now wrapped to a minimum of 6 full   circumferential passes at 50% overlap per our updated SWI-PKG-007.
- Verification: We will photograph the completed wrap on the first three post-corrective   shipments to your facility and include the photos with the packing slip.

Please find our formal corrective action report attached.

Best regards,
Marcus Tran
Quality Contact
Alloy Direct Supply

emails.subject: Q1 Audit Closed — Receiving Operations — Meridian
emails.body_text:
Jordan and Darnell,

The Q1 internal audit of Meridian receiving operations is now closed.

Sample: 22 receipts reviewed (January–March 2026).

Findings:
- 1 minor discrepancy: NCR closure timestamp on REC-20260225-009 (log shows 03-05,   email chain shows 03-04). Cross-shift correction per §13.6 will follow.
- 2 self-reported timing breaches — both handled correctly.
- All safety-critical receipts had COC, MTR, and heat number on file.
- No missing NCRs identified.
- No unauthorized quarantine releases.

Overall: Receiving operations are SOP-compliant. Full report going to Elizabeth and Walter by end of week.

Theo Brandt
Quality Manager
Gear Systems Inc. — Meridian Plant
